title: Salty Tender Midnight Craving French Raisin Stir-fry
description: A savory and tender French dish infused with the sweetness of raisins, perfect for midnight cravings and low in fodmap-friendly components.
instructions: Slice the pork tenderloin into thin strips. Pound them lightly with a mallet or rolling pin for tenderness.
In a small bowl, mix together olive oil, soy sauce, and fish sauce (if using). Add red pepper flakes if you like it spicy.
Place the pork in an oven-proof dish and pour the marinade over evenly. Marinate for at least 10 minutes or longer for better flavor integration.
Arrange the raisins on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Bake them until golden brown, about 8-10 minutes, ensuring they don't burn.
Remove the pork from the marinade and pat dry with paper towels. Heat some olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium-high heat.
Add garlic to the pan and sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant but not browned. Add the marinated pork, raisins, and remaining marinade.
Stir-fry everything until the pork is cooked through (internal temperature should reach at least 145°F) and most of the liquid has evaporated.
Serve hot as a snack or side dish.
